docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before proceeding with the installation, ensure that you have Docker and Docker Compose installed on your system. To do so you can follow the instructions provided on the docker's website.

docs.title: 1. Copy  Docker Compose File
docs.text:
Create a directory for your project and create a file named docker-compose.yml. Copy the provided service configurations into this file.

docker-compose.yaml

version: "3.9"

services:
  escalation:
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/escalation:latest
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      - rabbitmq
    extra_hosts:
      - "mongoservice:172.17.0.1"
      - "rabbitmqservice:172.17.0.1"
    environment:
      MONGOURL: "mongoservice:27017"
      RABBITURL: "amqp://alertagility:[RABBIT_MQ_PASSWORD]@rabbitmqservice:5673/alertagility"

  finEscalation:
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/findescalation:latest
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      - rabbitmq
    extra_hosts:
      - "mongoservice:172.17.0.1"
      - "rabbitmqservice:172.17.0.1"
    environment:
      MONGOURL: "mongoservice:27017"
      RABBITURL: "amqp://alertagility:[RABBIT_MQ_PASSWORD]@rabbitmqservice:5673/alertagility"

  alertagility:
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/alertagility:latest
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    depends_on:
      - rabbitmq
    extra_hosts:
      - "mongoservice:172.17.0.1"
      - "rabbitmqservice:172.17.0.1"
    environment:
      MONGOURL: "mongoservice:27017"
      DOMAIN: "demo.yourdomain.com"
      RABBITURL: "amqp://alertagility:wq:[RABBIT_MQ_PASSWORD]@rabbitmqservice:5673/alertagility"

  rabbitmq:
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/inithive-rabbitmq:latest
    restart: always
    ports:
      - "5673:5672"
    environment:
      - RABBITMQ_PASSWORD=[RABBIT_MQ_PASSWORD]
      - RABBITMQ_USER=alertagility

  mongodb:
    image: mongo:5
    network_mode: "host"
    volumes:
      - ./data:/data/db

  alert-frontend:
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/alertagility-frontend:v2
    restart: always
    depends_on:
      - alertagility
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    environment:
      - DOLLAR=$$
      - ALERTAGILITY_API=alertagility:9090
      - ALERTAGILITY_REPORT=alertagility:9191
      - VAULT_URL=`vault_host:port`
Find DOMAIN and replace with your actual domain name and adjust environments according to your system

docs.title: 2. Start Services
docs.text:
Open a terminal window, navigate to the directory where the docker-compose.yml file is located, and run the following command: 

docker compose up -d

This command will start all the services in detached mode, meaning they will run in the background.

docs.title: 3. Verify Installation
docs.text:
After running the docker-compose up -d command, you can verify that the services are running by executing:

docker compose ps

This command will display the status of all the services defined in the docker-compose.yml file.

docs.title: 4. Accessing the Application
docs.text: Once the services are up and running, you can access the frontend application by opening a web browser and navigating to http://localhost:3000.

docs.title: 5. Stopping Services
docs.text:
To stop the running services, execute the following command:

docker compose down

This will stop and remove all containers defined in the docker-compose.yml file.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The default username to access the frontend is admin@localhost
  • The default password is password
  • The services are configured to automatically restart (restart: always) in case of failures or system reboots.
  • The depends_on directive is used to define service dependencies, ensuring that dependent services are started before the dependent ones.
  • Ensure that your system meets the resource requirements for running the services, especially for memory and CPU.
  • You can customize environment variables and port mappings in the docker-compose.yml file as needed.

With these instructions, you should be able to successfully download and install the PixelView Services on your system. If you encounter any issues or have questions, refer to the Docker and Docker Compose documentation or feel free to ask for assistance.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before integrating the PixelView service with your OpenStack environment, ensure that you have the following prerequisites:

  • Access to an OpenStack environment (with Keystone authentication enabled).
  • Docker installed on your system.
  • Python: Follow the instructions provided on Python's website

docs.title: Generate the secret key
docs.text:
1. Open a new terminal

  2. Copy and paste the following command to run the Python interpreter in the terminal 

    python3

  3. In the terminal write the following script to generate a secure key 

    import secrets
secrets.token_urlsafe(32)

  4. You should get a key that looks similar to this Generated secret

    'sSlwF4DCqKTVtNIrp6DixVHblKWgqjYkSpVnvvjM1i8'
    Copy and store it somewhere safe as it will be used later on.

    Note

    Generating secret key using the provided method is not the only way or mandatory by any means. You can use your own combination of string, but make sure that it is strong enough so that it cannot be generated easily

docs.title: Configure the env file
docs.text:
Create a file named .env in the same directory as your docker-compose.yml file. This file should contain environment variables required for the openstack_mon service.

Here's an example .env file based on the provided information: .env

ALLOWED_ORIGINS="*"
DB_USERNAME="[OPENSTACK_DB_USERNAME]"
DB_PASSWORD="[OPENSTACK_DB_PASSWORD]"
DB_HOST="[OPENSTACK_DB_HOST]"
DB_PORT=[RUNNING_DB_PORT]
ROOT_URL="[URL:PORT]"
SECRET_KEY="[SECRET_KEY]" # Paste the generated key here
Replace the [SECRET_KEY] with the key generated in Step 1

docs.title: Pull Docker Image
docs.text:
Pull the latest version of the monitoring_openstack docker image by running the following command: 

docker pull ghcr.io/pixelvirt/monitoring_openstack:latest

docs.title: Create Docker Compose File
docs.text:
Create a docker-compose.yml file with the following content: docker-compose.yml

version: '3'
services:
  openstack_mon:
    restart: always
    image: ghcr.io/pixelvirt/monitoring_openstack:latest
    env_file:
      - .env
    ports:
      - "5000:5000"    
    container_name: openstack_mon
    command: "gunicorn main:app -b 0.0.0.0:5000 --reload --log-level=debug --log-file=api.log"

docs.title: Start the Service
docs.text:
Run the following command to start the monitoring_openstack service: 

docker compose up -d

docs.title: Configure OpenStack Integration
docs.text: In your monitoring application or system, configure the OpenStack integration using the API endpoint provided by openstack_mon. Provide the necessary authentication details and endpoints to enable monitoring of your OpenStack environment.

docs.title: Verify Integration
docs.text: Once the integration is configured, verify that your monitoring system can successfully retrieve metrics and monitor your OpenStack environment using the provided API.

docs.title: Conclusion
docs.text: By following these steps and providing the necessary environment variables, you can integrate the PixelView with your OpenStack environment and monitor it effectively. If you encounter any issues during the integration process, refer to the documentation of monitoring_openstack or seek assistance from the service provider.
